---
title: "Results"
subtitle: "Complete comparison across all methods"
---

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
metrics = FileAttachment("_data/combined_metrics.json").json()
```

## Validation era (2006-2015)

All results below are from the **rolling pseudo-real-time validation** with 120 monthly forecast origins.

### Summary table

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof resultMetric = Inputs.radio(
  ["rmse", "mae", "mase"],
  {label: "Metric", value: "rmse"}
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
{
  const mk = `avg_${resultMetric}`;
  const horizons = [1, 3, 6, 12];

  const rows = metrics.methods.map(m => {
    const row = {Method: m.display_name, Category: m.category};
    for (const h of horizons) {
      row[`h=${h}`] = m.summary[String(h)]?.[mk]?.toFixed(3) || "—";
    }
    return row;
  });

  return Inputs.table(rows, {
    sort: "h=12",
    columns: ["Method", "Category", ...horizons.map(h => `h=${h}`)],
    width: {Method: 180, Category: 90}
  });
}
```

### Heatmap

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| label: fig-heatmap
//| fig-cap: "Performance heatmap — all methods, variables, and horizons. Darker green = better (lower error)."

{
  const mk = resultMetric;
  const vars = ["cpi", "industrial_production", "retail_sales", "unemployment"];
  const horizons = [1, 3, 6, 12];

  const data = metrics.methods.flatMap(m =>
    vars.flatMap(v =>
      horizons.map(h => {
        const val = m.metrics[v]?.[String(h)]?.[mk];
        return {
          method: m.display_name,
          variable: v.replace(/_/g, " "),
          horizon: h,
          value: val || null,
          label: `h=${h}`
        };
      })
    )
  ).filter(d => d.value != null);

  // Compute cell key for faceting
  const cells = data.map(d => ({
    ...d,
    cell: `${d.variable} h=${d.horizon}`
  }));

  return Plot.plot({
    height: 350,
    marginLeft: 180,
    marginBottom: 50,
    padding: 0.05,
    x: {
      label: null,
      domain: vars.flatMap(v => horizons.map(h => `${v.replace(/_/g, " ")} h=${h}`)),
      tickRotate: -45,
      tickSize: 0
    },
    y: {
      label: null,
      domain: metrics.methods.map(m => m.display_name).reverse()
    },
    color: {
      scheme: "greens",
      reverse: true,
      label: mk.toUpperCase(),
      legend: true
    },
    marks: [
      Plot.cell(cells, {
        x: "cell",
        y: "method",
        fill: "value",
        tip: true,
        title: d => `${d.method}\n${d.variable} h=${d.horizon}\n${mk}: ${d.value.toFixed(4)}`
      })
    ]
  });
}
```


### Per-variable breakdown

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof detailVar = Inputs.select(
  ["cpi", "industrial_production", "retail_sales", "unemployment"],
  {label: "Variable", format: v => v.replace(/_/g, " ").replace(/\b\w/g, c => c.toUpperCase())}
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| label: fig-detail-bars
//| fig-cap: "Method comparison for the selected variable across horizons."

{
  const mk = resultMetric;
  const horizons = [1, 3, 6, 12];

  const data = metrics.methods.flatMap(m =>
    horizons.map(h => ({
      method: m.display_name,
      horizon: `h=${h}`,
      value: m.metrics[detailVar]?.[String(h)]?.[mk] || 0
    }))
  );

  return Plot.plot({
    height: 350,
    marginLeft: 60,
    marginBottom: 50,
    x: {label: null},
    y: {label: mk.toUpperCase(), grid: true},
    fx: {label: null},
    color: {legend: true},
    marks: [
      Plot.barY(data, Plot.groupX({y: "identity"}, {
        x: "method",
        y: "value",
        fill: "method",
        fx: "horizon",
        tip: true
      }))
    ]
  });
}
```


## Key findings

1. **ARIMA is the benchmark to beat** — best classical method at all horizons
2. **Zero-shot Chronos-2 is competitive at h=1** but deteriorates at longer horizons
3. **Seasonal naive is the weakest** — naive seasonality doesn't capture the dynamics well
4. **Random walk remains a strong baseline** especially at h=12

::: {.callout-note}
## Test era results
Test era (2016+) results with subperiod analysis (pre-COVID, COVID, post-COVID) will appear here after Phase 5 evaluation.
:::
